what_it_does: Records ledger metadata for a design-assets channel icon image named "image (14)", referencing an image/png blob, with dimensions 0x0, version 1, empty alt text, and no links.
observations: The asset is an image/png but has width and height set to 0
The alt text in the version block is empty
There is only one version and no links
Created and updated timestamps are identical
standouts: Zero width and height for an image asset
Empty alt text on a visual asset
Single-version entry with no outgoing links
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, channel, path, blob, mime, and version fields
References media via a blob:// URI
Maintains a versions array with per-version metadata
concerns: 0x0 dimensions suggest the image was not fully ingested or analyzed
Missing alt text limits accessibility and discoverability
No links or contextual fields describe where or how the icon is used
notes: A bare ledger stub for a design-assets icon whose image dimensions and descriptive metadata have not been populated.
questions: Why are the width and height recorded as 0?
What icon or concept does "image (14)" represent?
Why is the alt text empty?
Where is this icon intended to be used?
purpose_vs_actual: The file is meant to catalog a design-assets icon image, but it currently functions more like a placeholder because key image metadata (dimensions, alt text, links) is missing.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to register an icon image from the design-assets channel into the ledger so it can be referenced and versioned, though ingestion looks incomplete.
contribution: Provides an addressable, versioned metadata record that links a named icon to a specific blob, supporting future retrieval, linking, and lifecycle tracking within the design-assets channel.
summary_for_embedding: A channel-memory ledger entry for a design-assets icon image named "image (14)" referencing an image/png blob with zero recorded dimensions, version 1, empty alt text, and no links.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog a design-assets icon image in the ledger for later reference and reuse.
